# Responsible AI Statement

_UK GDPR required document template. Replace all [placeholders] with your organisation details before publication._

**Source:** `External - Customer-Facing Documents/Responsible AI Statement.docx`

---

Responsible AI Statement

## Uk Gdpr

Purpose

Defines publication controls for [Company Name]'s public Responsible AI commitments for [Product Name].

Aligns customer expectations with AI Governance Policy, Human Oversight Framework, and operational controls.

### The objective of this document is to ensure that

Communicate fairness, safety, transparency, and accountability principles.

State prohibited uses and feedback channels.

Ensure public commitments do not contradict internal AI Auditability Standard.

### This document supports

AI Governance Policy

Human Oversight Framework

AI Transparency Statement

AI Incident Procedure

AI Auditability Standard

Hallucination Mitigation Strategy

Scope

This document governs Applies to all optional AI and STT features offered in [Product Name] UK production. for [Company Name] in relation to the [Product Name] platform.

Territorial scope: United Kingdom (England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland) only.

Platform scope: multi-tenant SaaS hosted in Microsoft Azure UK South; excludes non-production sandboxes unless explicitly stated.

Non-clinical, human-in-the-loop assistive use only.

[Product Name] is a non-medical, non-clinical workspace platform (notes, work boards, teams, search, optional AI and STT). It does not provide diagnosis, treatment, or clinical decision support.

Production customer personal data SHALL remain in Microsoft Azure UK South only; no multi-region tenancy or data residency selection is offered.

Regulatory Alignment

This document implements [Company Name] accountability under UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018.

Compliance MUST be demonstrated with operational records assignable to named owners — not policy text alone.

Accountability under Article 5(2) and Article 24 UK GDPR.

Control Objectives

Statement MUST align with AI Governance Policy and Human Oversight Framework without contradiction.

Material changes MUST be approved by Executive Sponsor and DPO before publication.

Governance Structure

Governance Authority

Governance authority resides with [Company Name] Executive leadership, the Data Protection Officer, Information Security, and Engineering.

Material exceptions and residual risks require Executive or DPO approval as specified in this document.

Roles and Responsibilities

Function

Responsibility

Data Protection Officer

UK GDPR compliance review, lawful basis validation, ICO liaison

Legal

Drafting, regulatory wording, version control, contract alignment

Product

Feature accuracy, in-app notices, AI labelling

Engineering

Technical accuracy of data flows, retention, and security statements

Customer Success

Enterprise customer queries and controller/processor clarification

Security Lead

Article 32 statements, whitepaper accuracy, incident coordination

Public principles

Transparency — users know when AI is used; see AI Transparency Statement

Human oversight — users review outputs before operational reliance

Safety — abuse prevention, rate limits, and incident response per AI Incident Procedure

Fairness — monitoring for disproportionate harm; no discriminatory purpose by design

Privacy — UK South processing; no training on Customer workspace content

Accountability — governance board review for material AI changes

Prohibited uses (published)

Clinical reliance, diagnosis, treatment recommendations, or regulated medical advice

Solely automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effects

Circumventing tenant isolation or extracting other organisations' data via AI

Using [Product Name] AI as a substitute for qualified professional judgment without review

Feedback and incident channels

Feedback: privacy@[company-domain] and support@[company-domain].

Serious harm reports MUST escalate per AI Incident Procedure within 4 hours.

Statement MUST reference AI Transparency Statement for feature-level detail.

Internal alignment matrix

Public commitment

Internal document

Human review before reliance

Human Oversight Framework

Accuracy limitations

Hallucination Mitigation Strategy

Output checks

AI Output Validation Procedure

No training on Customer data

AI Data Usage Statement

Technical controls

AI Auditability Standard

Publication and commitment controls

Mandatory controls

Responsible AI Statement MUST align with AI Governance Policy and Human Oversight Framework without contradiction.

Statement MUST prohibit clinical reliance, diagnosis, treatment recommendations, and regulated medical advice.

Statement MUST prohibit solely automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effects.

Statement MUST require human review before operational reliance on AI outputs.

Statement MUST NOT market AI as human-equivalent or infallible.

Version and date MUST appear on published page.

Technical and privacy commitments

Mandatory controls

Statement MUST describe fairness and abuse prevention at high level (rate limits, monitoring).

Statement MUST state UK South processing and no training on Customer content.

Feedback channel (privacy@[company-domain] and support@[company-domain]) MUST be published.

Accessibility of AI features MUST be considered in product design commitments.

Environmental or cost disclosures MAY be included at Product discretion with Legal review.

Review and incident alignment

Mandatory controls

Statement MUST be reviewed annually and before material AI capability releases.

Material changes MUST be approved by Executive Sponsor and DPO.

Statement MUST reference AI Transparency Statement for feature-level detail.

Incident reporting for serious AI harm MUST reference AI Incident Procedure.

Internal mapping to risk register MUST be maintained.

Operational Procedure

The following procedure SHALL be executed for every in-scope event. Deviations require DPO approval.

1. AI Governance drafts or updates statement.

2. DPO reviews privacy and rights content.

3. Legal reviews enforceability of commitments.

4. Product confirms UX matches statements (labels, confirmations).

5. Executive Sponsor approves material commitments.

6. Publish to trust centre and website.

7. Link from [Product Name] AI settings and onboarding.

8. Communicate to Customer Success for enterprise FAQs.

9. Archive prior version.

10. Schedule annual review in compliance calendar.

Monitoring and Assurance

Monitoring demonstrates continuous operation of controls.

Control area

Expected evidence

Published statement

Version and date

Annual review record

DPO and Executive sign-off

AI release alignment

Change tickets referencing statement

Feedback log

AI concern themes quarterly

Risk register links

Responsible AI risks tracked

Escalation

Serious harm reports MUST escalate per AI Incident Procedure within 4 hours.

Exceptions and Deviations

### Exceptions MUST

be documented with business justification,

include compensating controls and risk assessment,

name an approval authority (DPO or Executive),

include an expiry date not exceeding 90 days unless renewed.

Expired exceptions SHALL be remediated, renewed, or formally closed within five working days of expiry.

Review and Maintenance

### This document SHALL be reviewed

at least annually,

before material AI capability releases,

after AI Incident Procedure activations affecting public commitments,

after ICO or regulatory AI guidance updates.

_Template — customize confidentiality and ownership statements for your organisation before distribution._
